question: The efficient market hypothesis argues
options: The primary purpose of a firm is to maximise returns to shareholders
Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity
Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
Asset prices fully reflect all available information
answer: Asset prices fully reflect all available information

question: Behavioural economics challenges
options: Income inequality within a country
Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity
The assumption that consumers always make rational decisions
How money flows between households and firms in an economy
answer: The assumption that consumers always make rational decisions

question: A negative externality occurs when
options: Asset prices fully reflect all available information
A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates
A third party bears costs from a transaction they are not part of
A new product or service that fundamentally changes an existing market
answer: A third party bears costs from a transaction they are not part of

question: The Gini coefficient measures
options: Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity
Using new investors' money to pay returns to earlier investors
Income inequality within a country
A third party bears costs from a transaction they are not part of

question: Tobin's Q ratio compares
options: A third party bears costs from a transaction they are not part of
A new product or service that fundamentally changes an existing market
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
How money flows between households and firms in an economy
answer: The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets

question: Dynamic pricing involves
options: Adjusting prices in real time based on demand and competition
Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions
How money flows between households and firms in an economy
Income inequality within a country
answer: Adjusting prices in real time based on demand and competition

question: The concept of bounded rationality means
options: Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
The assumption that consumers always make rational decisions
Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
answer: Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity

question: Regulatory capture occurs when
options: Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions
A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
Adjusting prices in real time based on demand and competition
answer: A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates

question: The circular flow of income model shows
options: The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions
How money flows between households and firms in an economy
answer: How money flows between households and firms in an economy

question: Shareholder value theory argues
options: The primary purpose of a firm is to maximise returns to shareholders
Using new investors' money to pay returns to earlier investors
Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates
answer: The primary purpose of a firm is to maximise returns to shareholders

question: Stakeholder theory argues
options: Adjusting prices in real time based on demand and competition
Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions
Asset prices fully reflect all available information
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
answer: Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions

question: A Ponzi scheme involves
options: Firms must balance the interests of all groups affected by their actions
Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
The primary purpose of a firm is to maximise returns to shareholders
Using new investors' money to pay returns to earlier investors
answer: Using new investors' money to pay returns to earlier investors

question: The theory of comparative advantage states
options: A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates
Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost
The assumption that consumers always make rational decisions
Decision-makers are limited by available information and cognitive capacity
answer: Countries should specialise in goods they produce at lowest opportunity cost

question: Lean production aims to
options: Eliminate waste and improve efficiency in the production process
The assumption that consumers always make rational decisions
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
A third party bears costs from a transaction they are not part of
answer: Eliminate waste and improve efficiency in the production process

question: Disruptive innovation refers to
options: A new product or service that fundamentally changes an existing market
A regulatory agency begins to act in the interest of the industry it regulates
The market value of a firm to the replacement cost of its assets
Asset prices fully reflect all available information
answer: A new product or service that fundamentally changes an existing market
